methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Asylon Robotics is a Series B stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $40M to date. The valuation universe comprises seven private companies in the sector, including peers such as Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero. No public comparables exist in this narrowly defined space.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($40M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $76M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier datapoints within the Drones & Autonomous cohort; while four same-sector peers exist, none met the filtering threshold of three or more comparable transactions.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $65M (low) to $87M (high), with a median of $76M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent arm's-length Drones & Autonomous comparable transactions at similar stages and valuation tiers, and reliance on a single methodological input. Additional data on recent Series B fundings in adjacent autonomous systems segments or on Asylon's revenue, burn rate, or deployment traction would strengthen conviction in this estimate.
